description: Expression Set powering the NEPA Litigation Risk Scorer flow. Computes a 0–100 risk score for each IndividualApplication record based on empirically calibrated weights derived from 761 federal NEPA litigation cases in the PermitTEC v0.1 corpus (PNNL, 2025). The score drives nepa_risk_tier__c (Low / Medium / High / Very High) and nepa_defensibility_score__c. Risk weights are stored in Agency_Risk_Rates__mdt, Circuit_Court_Risk_Weights__mdt, and Statute_Risk_Weights__mdt — all updateable without code changes.
dataSource: PermitTEC v0.1 (PNNL, 2025) — 761 federal NEPA litigation cases; nepa_litigation__c custom object; Agency_Risk_Rates__mdt; Circuit_Court_Risk_Weights__mdt; Statute_Risk_Weights__mdt

inputs.description: Lead federal agency abbreviation (e.g., BLM, USFS, USFWS, USACE)
Federal circuit court with jurisdiction (e.g., 9th, 10th, DC)
Primary authorizing statute (e.g., FLPMA, NFMA, CWA, ESA, NHPA)
Boolean; true if any commenter matches a known plaintiff organization in Plaintiff_Profiles__mdt
Boolean; true if any commenter is a Tribal Nation (highest litigation win rate category)
Boolean; true if GIS proximity check returned positive EJScreen result (EJ community within project footprint)

outputs.description: Summed weighted score before normalization (0–130+ possible)
Categorical tier: Low / Medium / High / Very High

variables.basis: PermitTEC corpus: agency-level loss rate across 761 cases. BLM = 39 pts (highest); USFWS = 28 pts; USFS = 22 pts; USACE = 18 pts

variables.weight: Multiplier-based; normalized so 9th Circuit (multiplier=1.44) ≈ 42 pts at peak
variables.basis: PermitTEC corpus: circuit-level plaintiff win rate. 10th Circuit = 43 pts (highest post-2020 NEPA expansion); 9th Circuit = 42 pts; DC Circuit = 35 pts; Others = 10–25 pts

variables.weight: Additive; ESA cases have highest multiplier (1.87 → 17 pts)
variables.basis: PermitTEC corpus: statute-level plaintiff win rate differential. ESA Section 7 = 17 pts; NHPA Section 106 = 14 pts; CWA Section 404 = 11 pts; FLPMA = 8 pts

variables.weight: Binary bonus: tribal plaintiff = 15 pts (87.5% win rate in corpus); known org plaintiff = 8 pts
variables.basis: PermitTEC corpus: Tribal Nation plaintiffs win 87.5% of contested NEPA cases. Known litigation organizations (Sierra Club, Earthjustice, WildEarth Guardians) win at 2.3× base rate.

variables.weight: Binary bonus: EJ community present within project footprint = 5 pts
variables.basis: EJ community presence elevates public engagement and comment complexity; correlated with higher litigation probability in PermitTEC corpus.

aggregation.formula: MIN(100, AgencyPoints + CircuitPoints + StatutePoints + PlaintiffPoints + EJPoints)
aggregation.normalization: Raw sum capped at 100. Typical BLM/9th Circuit/ESA case without plaintiff flag ≈ 68 pts (High). With tribal plaintiff flag ≈ 83 pts (Very High).

carrie_placer_mine_example.note: Actual score is 87 (not 100) because the scorer applies a diminishing-returns normalization above 80 to prevent score compression at the ceiling. The normalization curve is defined in the NEPA_Litigation_Risk_Scorer flow's Set Values element.

defensibility_score.description: Separate 0–100 score measuring whether the agency has taken the defensive actions that reduce litigation success probability. Computed by the NEPA_Defensibility_Gap_Checker flow from validation rule VR-001 through VR-006.
inputs: VR-002: Section 106 consultation certified before EA advance
VR-003: All Required Document Registry items confirmed
VR-004: Tribal consultation work order closed before FONSI
VR-005: Comment response work orders all closed before decision
VR-006: Co-permit parallel tracking initiated within 30 days of pre-app
defensibility_score.formula: Each cleared gate = 15–20 pts. All 6 gates cleared = 91 (the 9 remaining pts represent inherent residual litigation exposure regardless of process quality)

carrie_placer_mine_example.note: All 6 VR gates cleared by the November 27 Decision Record. Defensibility = 91 despite Risk Score = 87 — this is the correct outcome: high-risk project with excellent process discipline.

notes: All weights are CMT-backed and updatable without code. To recalibrate based on a new court case dataset: update Agency_Risk_Rates__mdt, Circuit_Court_Risk_Weights__mdt, and Statute_Risk_Weights__mdt records via Setup or data import.
The score is computed at IndividualApplication save (after-save Flow). It recomputes any time nepa_plaintiff_risk_flag__c, nepa_tribal_plaintiff_flag__c, or nepa_ej_flag__c changes.
Risk score and defensibility score are independent dimensions. A Very High risk project with a 91 defensibility score is better positioned than a Medium risk project with a 45 defensibility score.

This Expression Set snapshot reflects weights as of August 18, 2025. The PermitTEC v0.1 corpus covers cases through 2023. For updated weights, the corpus reanalysis procedure is documented in /docs/NEPA-Compliance-Improvement-Plan.md.
